refinement_instructions: Do not answer from preprocess alone. The answer-critical gap is the exact identity of the shot two shots before the second reviewed play and the count of complete flag-wave motions by the spectator in that shot. Preserve only broad preprocess anchors that there is an early review around 29-57s and another later review around 437-467s; the later one is the likely second reviewed play but must be grounded by the new evidence. Replace any unsupported assumption about which crowd shot is being referenced. The decisive evidence should come from the localized second review context plus direct inspection of the candidate spectator shot. If the new evidence does not clearly isolate the shot order or does not permit a reliable wave count, leave the answer unresolved rather than guessing.
